UNLAWFUL DETAINER MOTION 501, PLAINTIFF JOHN MORIARTY'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO BE BROUGHT WHEN ACTION IS RESOLVED. UNDER THE LEASE ONLY PREVAILING PARTY IN AN ACTION TO ENFORCE THE TERMS HEREOF OR RELATING TO THE DESCRIBED PREMISES MAY BE AWARDED ATTORNEY'S FEES. AUTHORITIES CITED FOR THE FIRST TIME IN REPLY (TURNER AND ACOSTA) ARE DISTINGUISHABLE. TURNER AND ACOSTA DEALT WITH ENFORCEMENT OF A DISCRETE CONTRACTUAL PROVISION FOR ARBITRATION. TURNER AND ACOSTA DO NOT STAND FOR THE PROPOSITION THAT ANTI-SLAPP MOTION IS A DISCRETE PROCEEDING ON THE CONTRACT OR THAT ATTORNEY'S FEES MAY BE AWARDED IN CONNECTION WITH ANTI-SLAPP UNDER THE CONTRACT. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT PROPOSED ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE A. JAMES ROBERTSON, CLERK: M. OLOPERNES, NOT REPORTED

MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES
Off Calendar May-14-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: PLAINTIFF JOHN MORIARTY's MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES is DENIED without prejudice to be brought when action is resolved. Under the Lease only prevailing party in an action to enforce the terms hereof or relating to the described premises may be awarded attorney's fees. Authorities cited for the first time in Reply (Turner and Acosta) are distinguishable. Turner and Acosta dealt with enforcement of a discrete contractual provision for arbitration. Turner and Acosta do not stand for the proposition that Anti-SLAPP motion is a discrete proceeding on the contract or that attorney's fees may be awarded in connection with Anti-SLAPP under the contract. Prevailing party to submit proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge A. James Robertson, Clerk: M. Olopernes, Not reported (D501)
